Cumberland - No Ordinary Bank. 

I had Jess  as my mortgage advisor and she was incredible, helpful and understanding, fantastic at keeping me in the loop and answering any questions. 

This bank (and Jess) have gone above and beyond - good old fashioned banking who care about you and your own merit, not a spreadsheet or AI deciding if your a fit for them!

I would highly recommend Cumberland if you’re looking at getting a mortgage when other lenders wont!

reviews.title: Very satisfied customers
reviews.body:
Very satisfied customers
Straightforward, honest and clear.  Our dealings with Cat were everything we would hope and expect .  The difference is that so few companies give the service that we would like to expect, The Cumberland and in particular Cat do.  Whenever you phone, within a very short period of time you are speaking directly with a real person, and if they are not available they always ring back promptly. Any paperwork is straightforward and clear.  They treat people as they themselves would like to be treated, simple really but so few companies do that these days.

reviews.title: Nice people nasty process.
reviews.body:
Nice people nasty process.
The Cumberland has always been very good at customer relations, as well as providing a good interest rate and branch access. My only gripe is that my most recent engagement with them was far from straightforward. My one year regular saver had matured and I wanted to open another one. I have been a member for many years so it should be straightforward. Not at all. I had to undergo a lengthy telephone interview. Very nice helpful lady on the other end, but that did not make it altogether easy. Loads of bureaucratic legal stuff to navigate. I was supposed to read reams of this stuff, which may be great for lawyers, but I am a pensioner with a brain condition, who is unlikely to remember any of it. I agreed to receiving documents via the internet for us to sign and then had to work out how to operate my printer... Then a trip to the branch. Was it really worth the extra interest given the fact it only lasts a year and then I will have to do it all over again? Is it really necessary? I would be happy to sign a disclaimer along the lines of saying I haven't read all the details at my own risk.

reviews.title: Appalling Service
reviews.body:
Appalling Service
Penrith branch were most unhelpful when I tried to book a face to face appointment in advance of my account maturing, they told me I had to wait for a letter to arrive and then when I did get it there were no face to face appointments available and I was therefore left with a telephone appointment that I did not want.  

The person, Deep Singh, was helpful and arranged our account as requested but, unfortunately, when the documents arrived they contained several errors and had an empty box which had the word error all around it.  Therefore I had to then email and also telephone to arrange amendments before having to drive into town to collect the new documents for myself and my husband to sign.

Had we had a face to face appointment we would not have had all this trouble and matters would have been dealt with there and then.  At one point we very nearly withdrew our money due to the appalling service but due to the timescales and other matters we were dealing with at the time remained with the Cumberland in the hope that matters would improve in the future as they could not have been worse.

reviews.title: Bad service by Penrith branch
reviews.body:
Bad service by Penrith branch
Trying to get a face to face appointment was impossible even though we had tried well in advance of our account maturing being told we needed to wait for a letter.  When the letter finally arrived no appointments were available leaving us no choice but to have a telephone appointment.  This appointment was carried out by Deep Singh who we had no problem with and we arranged how we wanted our accounts going forward.  Unfortunately, when the details were emailed over they showed errors at our end so we then had to once again get in touch to try and correct this with us having to go into branch to collect the documents for our signatures before then having to return them once signed.  What could have been done in a one to one face appointment led to us being considerably inconvenienced and this resulted in us raising a complaint against those at the Penrith branch.  We even considered moving our savings to another provider as the service at the Penrith branch was appalling.  Matters were resolved when we spoke to an area Manager.

reviews.title: Difficulties in funding a recently opened account
reviews.body:
Difficulties in funding a recently opened account
Appointment on Thursday 29/08/25 for opening a new ISA account. went well, it was quick and straightforward.  CBS Advisor was pleasant and knowledgeable.

Issues arose when trying to fund the account the next day which was within the 14 day funding period.

I couldn't move the money electronically from my current account within Internet banking app.

I rang Customer Care to ask them to do the transfer.  Iinitially, was refused until they checked after I challenged them.

I decided the next day to add more money to the account, still within within the 14 day period.  Rang Customer Care and was met with the same response. Had to wait over the weekend to hear back from the staff member.  I was told the transfer could be done but this was the last day. It was clearly not the last day.

Customer Care staff should be made more aware of how this type of account operates initially.

reviews.title: WONDERFUL EXPERIENCE - THANK YOU
reviews.body:
WONDERFUL EXPERIENCE - THANK YOU
Visited Englist St Carlisle with intention of opening another ISA  Greeted by team member Erin & she was very helpful & I left with telephone appt scheduled with Shaun Veitch later that day! Shannon rang on time. He explained everything I needed to know in detail and read through all the essential terms & conditions. He said he'd complete all electronic documentation send them to CBS office nearer to my home for checking & signing by myself. I went to that branch & was served again by Erin & she remembered me which was nice. I checked, signed forms. Next I received by email account bo & terms/conditions from Liam Benson. I visited other BS transferred monies! And the following day I received my new ISA Account Book showing the funds had transferred successfully! Big thank you especially to Shaun Veitch for his approachability, guidance & efficiency & also he too remembered having dealt with me the previous year on a similar matter which was reassuring! Also, to Erin for her excellent interpersonal & administrative skills! The experience was made simple because of both your efforts!

reviews.body:
Changing types of accounts.
Although each individual advisor has tried to be helpful when we have changed our mortgage account & savings account, I have had to wait over 2 weeks to find that my current account ( no longer offset to our mortgage ) will remain the same account number. The advisors have told me to wait until a certain advisor is back from holiday before they can answer my query as to my current account number. 
The system seems very cumbersome & I would have thought that within 3 working days someone could have given me reassurance either that a new direct debit I wanted to set up would go to my current account if I used my present account number or given me the new account number.

Their interest rates are higher than other banks which is a shame. However, my mortgage advisor was UNBELIEVABLE, I delay with Jessica Hartley and honestly she was just amazing. She was always at the end of the phone, quick replies and appointments and managed to get my mortgage approved quickly and swiftly even with it being a fairly complex property involving land. reviews.title: Disappointing service
reviews.body:
Disappointing service
We visited the Cumberland branch 1st July as we each had an isa in the Nationwide maturing on 27th July 2025 and were looking to move to the Cumberland isa paying 4.2%.  After completing forms there was an issue with the account numbers so I contacted Nationwide who said that I had given the correct details for transfer.  We went on holiday and I thought things would be put in motion and hopefully all be sorted by the end of July.  However it is now the 12th August and still the money has not been moved to the Cumberland. So disappointing!!

reviews.title: Poor start, great finish
reviews.body:
Poor start, great finish
First contact less than satisfactory. First person I spoke to gave me information I knew was wrong about transferring in an Isa. I think it was his first day. First he put me on hold for a good few minutes and then when I queried something else he said he was putting me on hold and promptly cut me off. So why four stars? I spoke to two other staff who were right on it. The first made me an appointment  with the appropriate officer. She called me on time, sent me the mandate forms promptly and expedited the transfer from Barclays and sent my passbook in the post. First class service, especially welcome as I have ad some hair raising encounters with Barclays before.

reviews.title: Timing out
reviews.body:
Timing out
Overall very good. During my recent call with Amy from Carlisle customer services she was very helpful & patient with my lack of memory unfortunately due to this the security feature on my account timed out & we had to end the call to enable me to log back into the system Amy called me back and the requested transaction was completed.  Very pleasant and efficient service by Amy. A suggestion for senior post holders  - to give customers piece of mind the callback is genuine, before ending the first conversation agree a security question to be asked with the answer only being provided in the callback so the customer then knows they are talking to the Cumberland   I recognised Amy’s voice so only chastised myself for not thinking of this suggestion during our initial conversation

reviews.title: Disappointed.Painful process for SA mortgage-cannot recommend unfortunately
reviews.body:
Disappointed.Painful process for SA mortgage-cannot recommend unfortunately
It is with regret that I post negative feedback. Like many, I rarely post average but only good, and negative feedback. The process with Cumberland was painstakingly long. Whatever documentation was requested, I provided, through my broker. Whatever I provided never seemed enough, always more documentation was requested. I felt whatever I provided never seemed that it would satisfy this lender. Disappointingly, their ultimate offer was based on income net of all costs! Who does that? No provider I’ve used before has done this. I was desperate in the end and the time they took forced me with little choice but to go with their offer or start again with another lender. What a shame!
Excellent

Your colleagues in Haltwhistle were very friendly and helpful, but initially tried to direct me to Brampton to open my accounts.  I wasn't impressed with this you're now the only bank in town which is why I went to you, I didn't expect and wasn't prepared to be re-directed elsewhere.  Fortunately your colleague Kye intervened positively.

reviews.title: Good product - frustrating "faff" to sign up for another savings account.
reviews.body:
Good product - frustrating "faff" to sign up for another savings account.
While I understand regulations aimed at: 1. Ensuring customers fully understand what they are signing up for; and 2. Anti-money-laundering - it is frustrating to be treated like children when we have had 3 accounts with them for a number of years including one savings ISA exactly the same but a different issue, that they HAVE TO schedule and appointment with and advisor and the counter staff aren't able to take our word for it that we know what we are doing and the source of funds is obvious to them.  Having said that, the advisor on the phone was helpful and didn't take too long, and the savings rate was good.  They are also a mutual society.

This was my first experience going through the mortgage process alone and I was very nervous. Matthew Rooney explained every aspect of the process over the phone and having the option of fixing over two or five years. This gave me more choice than I had expected considering many other companies would not even consider me for a mortgage because of my personal circumstances being single and self employed. Matthew communicated via email asking for some additional information that was needed and where I was in the process which was reassuring. Matthew was very personable yet professional. reviews.title: Not upto Skipton standard.
reviews.body:
Not upto Skipton standard.
Went into Carlisle with a cheque to open a cash Isa, I was told there was nobody able to action it and i should make an appointment or if i had a mobile with me ring the place i was in and they would action it over the phone. I told they i was not happy opening an account over the phone, they suggested i should go to the Kingstown branch and they would do the job, i did this and they opened the account without any fuss, a few days later a letter came through my letter box and the account book fell out as the letter had been opened. I attempted to ring the Cumberland branch but got put through to another operator, i told him the story and that i was worried about fraud also the account book had only printed 3 letters of my surname I E Wat then faded out, the happy chap i rang at about 10.10Am Rang me back at about 4pm and said i should go into the Kingstown branch and they would print me a new book when i did go in they offered to put a label over the half printed name and hand write it in i refused and they printed a new book with just about readable name.
Not a very good experience. J K Waters.

The service I have received at the Ulverston branch has been very good. The advisor made the whole investment process very easy for me. She was friendly and reassuring. My only issue us that the office is only open 3 days a week. As I live some distance away I have to carefully plan visits so that I can do all my town jobs in one trip. If I find the branch is closed then I have to make another trip into town on another day. I do like face to face interaction rather than online or telephone banking.
Pleasant reliable staff. Both on the phone & in person. Good to have the actual building  & a person to chat to.
Excellent. Staff very helpful and sorted my query well

There is no other building society locally.  No choice.  I am not "tech savvy" whatever that means so I will not tolerate online banking until there is no choice.  Your Egremont branch was closed for over a month for redecorating but is still only open for three short days a week.  The new cash machines are very unreliable and the loss of mini statements disappointing.  You have a monopoly so get out there and advertise and GROW!

--Staff helpful and personable.
--On time and efficient.
--Explained what I needed.
--Unfortunately my surname spelt incorrectly on a forms/passbook so I asked for it to be changed. This meant sending it back to head office so I was unable to be given the passbooks needed. This seems quite an inefficient way of dealing with a spelling mistake!!
--I was disappointed and surprised that the Cumberland has no form of using bank card transfer from my bank, (or anyone elses) to simply transfer finances. After doing a 60 mile round trip from my home, I now have to do another 40 mile trip to my nearest bank to sey up the transfer of the funds. No reason was given except the facility usn't available. But why not. Its basic banking protocol surely?

reviews.title: Cumberland yes, Furness no
reviews.body:
Cumberland yes, Furness no
Julia was very helpful and I was happy that a trainee was also present.  Julia advised me on what account to take out.  The reason I have chosen Cumberland BS is that you have a branch in the next town to Seascale and also Whitehaven.  There are times when you want to speak face to face for help.  I was with Furness BS, it was in Gosforth, a few miles away but that shut down a few years ago.  When  I saw Egremont branch had reopened following a revamp I decided to transfer to Cumberland BS.  

Cumberland and Santander are the only banking available in Egremont and Whitehaven. Egremont approx 10 miles and Whitehaven approx 15 miles. 

Also having a person you can actually talk to rather than a chat with a computer is less stressful.

My statements arrive on time and are simple to follow and I love that I can go into a branch and will be helped with professionalism and a friendly manner by all the staff. I would totally recommend the Cumberland Building Society especially after comparing it with my old Bank services. Yes I'm a very happy customer, thank you to the lovely  staff and particularly the Maryport branch.

reviews.title: MUTUALLY BENEFICIAL  !
reviews.body:
MUTUALLY BENEFICIAL  !
Very professional and helpful.  It's so nice to deal with a Building Society that stays true to its founding principles and chooses to remain mutual. Furthermore it retains a high street presence offering the convenience of calling in and speaking face-to-face with someone.  Many other Building Societies have de-mutualised and closed their town branches, thereby forcing their customers to use the very impersonal internet with the ever-increasing risk of hacking and fraudulent activity.

reviews.title: Mortgage Advice - Hannah Williamson
reviews.body:
Mortgage Advice - Hannah Williamson
I cannot even begin to tell you how amazing Hannah Williamson was helping me with my mortgage application. I was going through a very difficult time with a separation when I first made contact with Hannah she was friendly, relatable and lovely to deal with explaining everything to me simply and answered any questions I may of had. Since then Hannah has been so efficient and speedy when answering all my questions and queries and moving my new mortgage application through without any issues and keeping me updated every step of the way during this stressful and challenging time for me. I would recommend Hannah time and time again to every person that is making an application. She was always positive and reliable and cannot thank her enough for the advice and support she has given me.

reviews.title: Excellent Customer Service
reviews.body:
Excellent Customer Service
The customer service was excellent with the representative making sure that we were fully informed of the terms and conditions which applied to the account that we were about to open.
The explanations were fulsome and easily understood. I was also impressed on the level of security surrounding our account application.  
There was plenty of time spent with us and at no time did we feel that there were time pressures. Very please with the whole experience, thank you.

Darcy Cannon provided clear, efficient in-person customer service. 
I would provide 5 stars for her, however, I need to take into account the previous 4 months of trying to do an online application, having a call then a delayed call and finally getting a clear answer from Darcy in April. 
The previous accounts person, William was friendly and tried to help (Feb 3rd). I explained my situation and my understanding of the potential hurdles to my application. He was unable to provide me with a clear and timely answer to my questions.
My recommendation: advise customers who do not have clear-cut situations to have an in-branch, in-person discussion about opening new accounts with someone who can answer all the questions there and then. Via phone/online is slow and inaccurate because info needs to be verified.

reviews.body:
Friendly but archaic system of operation.
The staff all seem efficient and courteous.
I very much appreciate the personal approach, especially the availability of local branches.
However, the process of opening and accessing accounts is laboured.
I already have two savings accounts, yet I had to have another telephone conversation (after two weeks of waiting) and jump through the same hoops again. To my mind, this is totally unnecessary.
I would like to monitor my accounts online from time to time and this is currently not possible unless I have a current account. Time this changed.
P.S. I don't appreciate the patronising comments below.

reviews.title: Good, but could be better...
reviews.body:
Good, but could be better...
The staff are always friendly and helpful. I like the fact that l can walk in to a local branch. 
I wish that I could be more enthusiastic about the Cumberland, but it lets itself down. It could be so much better.
The online banking is clunky and somewhat lacking in features. They need to update it and make it more user friendly. 
Their savings rates are average at best, which is disappointing. 
I also wish that there were more face to face appointments available, instead of over the telephone. 
An annual prompt to review finances would be much appreciated. I'm forever having to check rates to make sure that I'm getting the most out of my savings.
I really appreciate their security features, customer service and their 'local' links.

reviews.title: Re-mortgage heaven
reviews.body:

With the large high street bank it took them two weeks just to reply to an email. 

Cumberland are a credit and Sarah is a credit to The Cumberland.

reviews.title: After over 50 years with a distant account, it was time to change
reviews.body:
After over 50 years with a distant account, it was time to change
Had no problem opening a new current account on a Wednesday.  Within a week had received my new debit card, cheque book and pin.  I already had two isa savings accounts with the branch, so was not surprised by the good service.  Having had the same bank account in Edinburgh since I was a student in the 60s, it was now time to open an account near to my home, with easy access to staff.

I have been a member the Cumberland for many years. I visited the English Street branch to open a second Instant Access account. I was told that I would need to make an appointment with a Customer Advisor. One was not available at that time so a telephonic communication was arranged when Emma Symonds went over information that the Society would already have and regulations of which I am already aware or which I have already accepted. I was then sent an application form to sign and required to produce an identity document which the Society would already have. I do not see why I needed to be treated as though I was a new customer

From start to finish the Cumberland, specifically Leanne Irving offered an exemplary service for my new mortgage. It was absolutely no stress at all and I have already recommended to colleagues looking to move. Leanne went above and beyond and even kindly reassured me when she was supposed to be on leave. I found my dream home following a sudden change in personal circumstances, and Leanne was sympathetic of my situation and helped turned my dreams into reality. I couldn’t have done it without her and the financial support from the Cumberland. Thank you so much. I really do appreciate all your hard work Leanne!

reviews.title: Help starting an instant access and new regular saver accounts.Veryhelpfull
reviews.body:
Help starting an instant access and new regular saver accounts.Veryhelpfull
The meeting went well.
Full information was given about the instant access and the regular saver accounts. 
I hadn't stopped the automatic transfer of £250 monthly to my previous regular saver.
The saver changed automatically to the instant access account ( because it was the first time),  so £250 was invested in the instant access account.
David Tweedie noted this and was able to stop this, then, made it invest into my new regular saver account.
Thankfully I had a buffer of £250 in my current account (which I knew could be handy to have as a reserve).
This boosted my start up instant access account by £250. 
Overall a good meeting.
Good service

reviews.body:

It was all right.

reviews.title: The Cumberland as competent, professional and customer focussed as ever.
reviews.body:

The process was very straightforward and much less stressful than I had anticipated. 
I had been customer at Cumberland some years ago and was pleased that the staff maintained a very high level of competence, professionalism and customer service that I had previously experienced.

reviews.body:

Generally a positive experience. Attentive, prompt when I did need to speak…. For a new current account was asked for either a previous CA statement *or* employment contract. Regardless of this a bank statement was needed. Cheerfully dealt with by Customer Services - answered by an *actual human* answered within 2 mins. Wow !!!!🤩 

Only real negative - when my Internet Banking one-time activation passcode arrived, ‘your passcode’ was clearly visible through the letter window. Needs reformatted into a different place on the letter.

Staff very helpful & friendly.
Only annoying item is that the end of each month statement arrives around 8/9 of next month in post.

Always good service and helpful staff with quick pickup on the phone when calling with any query . I had my doubts about changing my current account from a bank to a building society but everything has been great and I can only praise every one from the Cumberland that I have had dealings with
Already completed a survey but reiterate.... excellent.

well fo me

reviews.title: Words fail me. !!!
reviews.body:
Words fail me. !!!
Staff fine. Visited branch Wed 29th January, gave details to open cash isa.Telephone appointment for Monday 3rd February. More details given and reference back to branch Wed 5th to sign documents. Awaiting pass book still on 10th Feb. Post arrived, no pass book.!!.Branch closed Tuesday, so if!!! Pass book comes by Wednesday, it will have taken 2 weeks to pay cheque in.??and 3 visits to Branch.!!!
Simple , easy way to open savings account. Joshua was great explaining all the options

reviews.title: I was disappointed
reviews.body:
I was disappointed
It proved very difficult to open a new ISA account.  I couldn't do it at my local branch  - it is only open three days a week and of the three people working there only one is able to open an account for me - except she couldn't as she was fully booked.    There was a limited span of time in which I could open an account (i.e. transfer money from an existing account) so I agreed to do so over the phone.  This though wasn't quite possible as I still had to sign a form - and this meant my travelling to the nearest branch that was open which was twelve miles away.

I may have opened a new ISA account but I haven't yet received a pass book. I signed the form on the last day of the year - the next day New Years Day was obviously a holiday - but on the 2nd, 3rd or 4th I expected a pass book to come through the post - it hasn't.

I didn't like being called by my Christian name when I was called - especially by someone I have never met.  This form, which is obviously computer generated, uses my surname and the title 'Mr' which I prefer.

The Cumberland is a good old fashioned building society and I would like it to succeed - I would recommend it - but my recommendation would be very qualified.

reviews.title: Very strong security
reviews.body:
Very strong security
Dear Feedback, your software is too quick to ask for an opinion, because I have not used the Cumberland Bank yet. 
I have opened two account numbers, and have received separate letters for each account that simply state the account number. 
Then, I receive the debit cards for each account in separate letters.
Then, I receive separate letters revealing pin numbers, but these letters do not reveal which account the pin numbers are for. 
This is very strong security indeed, which I will have to find a way to solve....

reviews.title: Good service and poor service.
reviews.body:
Good service and poor service.
Both good service and poor.
On Fri 20th Dec I rec’d a planned phone call re setting up a new savings account which was excellent - Charley Bennett was friendly, cheerful, knowledgable and helpful.
When I had questions the next morning I rang the help line and was told Charley was off work and someone else from her team would ring me back.
No one rang me so I rang again at 3.40 and was told I would be called within the hour.
No one rang so I called again about 4.45. I was told that I had been called at 4.02. That was incorrect, I had sat next to my phones for the full hour and neither my landline or mobile had rung. I was then told that Charley would be at work the next day and she would call me, which she did most efficiently.
Really helpful for first time buyers and the mortgage advisors are fantastic too in helping you every step of the way.

Very helpful,explained every thing to me and made sure l understood.

reviews.title: real people
reviews.body:
real people
the main thing for me is you talk to a real person sorry a helpful real person i went to see about savings accounts they even offer coffee sorry can't remember his name he was so helpful he showed me what was available for my savings 40 mins done and dusted and all available on line rang the nice lady at carlisle she helped me with getting set up on line every thing top notch but most of all its talking to a real person thank you very much the cumberland

reviews.title: AMAZING CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE
reviews.body:
AMAZING CUSTOMER EXPERIENCE
Recent input due to Bond maturing and wishing to open ISA using the Bond funds & transfer funds from another account outwith The Cumberland making up to the £20,000 annual limit. Had telephone consultation with Shaun @ English Street Branch Carlisle and the ISA Account is now open awaiting me to call into the Branch and transfer funds from other account, which I plan to do in the next few days. 
The whole procedure has been smooth. Shaun guided me effortlessly through the options open to me and when I'd decided he explained each step needed by both parties, which was a big help!
I also dealt with Ella and Sarah in The Branch and they were both very professional and helpful too!
Thank you everyone.  Keep up the good work! Also, just to add, I love the design & functionality of the Branch adaptations, very easy to manoeuvre to where you'll get assistance!
Merry Chrudtmas and a Healthy & Happy New Year to you all.
Kathleen Falder

reviews.title: Excellent service
reviews.body:
Excellent service
Excellent friendly and helpful service. I initially spoke with Alison at Workington who explained everything about opening my current account and savings account via a telephone appointment. Alison made the process very easy for me and was very clear and helpful. Going forward I’ll be dealing with the lovely staff in my local Kendal branch. Overall I couldn’t be happier with the service I’ve received from the Cumberland.

reviews.title: Great service and they still have local branches on the high street
reviews.body:
Great service and they still have local branches on the high street
Good service but it's a shame everything requires a chat with a savings advisor. I guess it's mandatory financial rules but I don't need advice to consider before I open a savings account.

Mr King, was really straight with me witch was appreciated, great bloke who is a credit to the Cumberland.!

Regards 
Allan G Graham

reviews.title: Switching to CBS was flawless. Had no issues.
reviews.body:
Switching to CBS was flawless. Had no issues.
The service is good. In future if I see anything that needs improvement, I will notify relevant department.
Good advice, polite and helpful and great we got a local branch.

Superb.
Recently dealing with fraud, which was horrible.  Couldn’t have had better help and support.
Excellent service could not fault anything. 9

reviews.title: Existing customer
reviews.body:
Existing customer
Easy to organise and in my case I had to rearrange, this was done with no fuss and with complete ease. The chat with the advisor was as I had expected, friendly and professional, with my query dealt with as I wished and to my satisfaction.

reviews.title: Another satisfied customer!
reviews.body:
Another satisfied customer!
I already had an account with the Cumberland and was happy to be able to open a second, by way of a transfer from another society

The whole procedure went smoothly and I was pleaded to meet the staff of your Rosehill branch.

Welcoming Building Society

reviews.title: Ecellent service and result

reviews.title: Excellent customer service - Wigton & Aspatria rural branches
reviews.body:
Excellent customer service - Wigton & Aspatria rural branches
Clare O'Brien our advisor was excellent she explained the account switch and the process reassuring me that all my direct debits would move over seamlessly.

We love the personal service and the fact you can email a real person directly instead of an automated call centre.

The Cumberland staff at Wigton and Aspatria branches are all very friendly and knowledgeable and we'd highly recommend anyone to switch and support local banking and branches which are imperative to rural communities.

The customer service has been excellent taking into consideration my husband's PTSD and social anxiety. Clare listened to our banking needs and gave us a personalised caring and efficient service. 

I love the Cumberland debit card to it's great to see local scenery captured as an image. Just another local personal touch.
Covered all relevant detail and explained clearly what to do.

reviews.title: Excellent service very easy to setup and no pressure to change what I hade.
reviews.body:

Service was excellent.I had a good idea of what I wanted when I went to my appointment so talked through the various options with a very helpful and friendly consultant.

reviews.title: A superb local branch. The reduction in opening hours is a slight cause for
reviews.body:
A superb local branch. The reduction in opening hours is a slight cause for

The telephone service for setting up a new account was also excellent. There is an awful lot to be said for organisations which are locally based and manned.

Excellent extremely quick, helpful and explained everything required and why.

reviews.title: Good products and helpful staff
reviews.body:
Good products and helpful staff
Good range of products with very friendly and helpful staff. Dumfries branch is convenient for us. Applications for new products (even as an existing customer) are a bit long winded and could be improved by introducing web based applications. As an example, it was far easier opening an ISA at Coventry BS than Cumberland BS.

reviews.title: Changing Banking to a personal service, hooray👏
reviews.body:
Changing Banking to a personal service, hooray👏
Very polite and sincere and helped me set up new account. It’s nice to have a Bank that’s local for the people with access for disabled. Went above and beyond. Nice to talk face to face with a professional and not online banking.
Thank you Cumberland Bank.
